focal_character.name: Aminata Diop
focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Aminata Diop is back at her desk at the Port of Dakar after the MV Sankofa has sailed, looking at the three requirements she wrote out separately from the consolidated instruction.

problem_structure.unknown: which clause of the compound customs condition actually binds and must be solved first
data: three folders sealed red for perishables, blue for transit goods, and black for dual-use materials
invoices, temperature logs, and phytosanitary certificates for perishables
transit manifests, waybills, and proof of bonded carrier status
a single dual-use sheet watermarked with a rifle over a wrench
problem_structure.condition: the consolidated instruction fuses three separate customs requirements into one sentence and demands all conditions be satisfied before the noon sailing

gate_details.correct_approach: Stop treating the instruction as a single requirement; tear the compound condition along its natural clause boundaries, set aside the parts that do not bind, and test each clause alone to find which one actually stalls the clearance.

new_texture: Supervisors drop consolidated clearance instructions directly on clerks' blotters with a noon sailing deadline.
The port uses Form C-7B for perishable cargo clearances.
Bonded transit documentation is required for non-perishable cargo moving through Senegal to landlocked Mali.
A new ECOWAS clause requires pre-inspection by an approved auditor for dual-use materials.
Industrial-grade lithium batteries used in forklifts also appear in drone power systems and trigger dual-use holds.
Auditors' deputies can review flagged dual-use holds remotely and lift them within minutes.

anchor_update.observation: Grade 2. System builder approach. Room organised by the solver's method — every container classified, every label aligned, the whole condition treated as one requirement. Underlined: the condition had three clauses; the solver's method could hold only one at a time. Register 1 throughout. No Register 2.
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor now leaves physical traces inside or near gates—pages that describe the mirror content and register behavior with increasing precision. Their notation explicitly identifies 'the condition had three clauses; the solver's method could hold only one at a time,' confirming they understand decomposition as the missing operation.

macro_mystery_update.evidence: A folded notebook page in the same concise notation as the Lagos and Accra observations is found tucked under the Dakar gate's threshold seal, recording the system builder approach and explicitly noting that the compound condition had three clauses while the solver's method could hold only one at a time.
